title: Subscription lifecycle with continue-as-new and timers
purpose: Long-running workflows that span months or years accumulate unbounded event history, degrading replay performance and memory usage. Developers need a concrete example showing how to use continue-as-new to periodically checkpoint state and start a fresh execution, combined with durable timers for periodic billing and signals for plan changes. This pattern applies to any subscription, lease, or recurring-schedule workflow.
task: Create a subscription-lifecycle example that defines a Gleam workflow using the aion_flow SDK. The workflow represents a monthly subscription: it sleeps for one billing period using timer.sleep(), executes a bill_subscriber activity, handles upgrade/downgrade signals, and after a configurable number of billing cycles calls workflow.continue_as_new() to keep event history bounded. Include a gleam.toml and a README explaining the continue-as-new pattern and long-lived workflow management.

requirements.title: Periodic billing with durable timers
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a workflow using workflow.define() that loops through billing cycles, sleeping for a configurable period using timer.sleep() between each cycle and executing a bill_subscriber activity on each iteration.
acceptance: subscription.gleam imports from aion_flow and uses workflow.define().
The workflow accepts configuration for billing period and max cycles before continue-as-new.
timer.sleep() is used to wait between billing cycles.
A bill_subscriber activity executes each cycle with the current plan and cycle number.
The workflow survives engine restart mid-sleep and resumes at the correct point in the billing cycle.

requirements.title: Signal handling for plan changes
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL use signal.receive() to listen for upgrade and downgrade signals concurrently with the billing timer. WHEN a plan-change signal arrives, THE SYSTEM SHALL update the subscription state for the next billing cycle.
acceptance: The workflow listens for a 'plan_change' signal using signal.receive().
concurrency.race() or equivalent is used to handle signals arriving during a billing sleep.
An upgrade signal changes the plan level for subsequent billing cycles.
A downgrade signal changes the plan level for subsequent billing cycles.
Plan changes are reflected in the next bill_subscriber activity execution.

requirements.title: Continue-as-new for history management
requirements.spec: WHEN the workflow has completed a configurable number of billing cycles, THE SYSTEM SHALL call workflow.continue_as_new() with the current subscription state as input, starting a fresh execution with clean event history.
acceptance: workflow.continue_as_new() is called after the configured max_cycles threshold.
The current subscription state (plan, cycle count, subscriber info) is passed as input to the new execution.
The new execution resumes billing from the correct cycle number.
Event history in the old execution is bounded to max_cycles worth of events.

requirements.title: Project setup and documentation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a gleam.toml with the aion_flow dependency and a README explaining the continue-as-new pattern, why history management matters for long-lived workflows, and step-by-step instructions for building and running the example.
acceptance: gleam.toml lists aion_flow as a dependency and has correct project metadata.
README.md explains why unbounded event history is a problem and how continue-as-new solves it.
README.md describes the subscription lifecycle including billing, plan changes, and history rotation.
README.md provides build and run instructions including how to send plan-change signals.

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the aion_flow SDK, engine, or server code — only consume them.
SHALL NOT add dependencies beyond aion_flow to the Gleam example.
SHALL NOT implement real billing or payment integrations — stub activities are sufficient.
SHALL NOT use real calendar durations — use short timer values (seconds) for demonstration purposes with a note about production values.
SHALL NOT handle cancellation in this example — cancellation patterns are a separate concern.
verification: gleam build succeeds in the subscription directory without warnings.
The .aion package can be built from the compiled source.
The workflow completes multiple billing cycles with timer.sleep() between each.
Sending a plan_change signal mid-cycle updates the plan for subsequent billing.
After max_cycles, the workflow calls continue_as_new and a new execution starts with carried-over state.
Event history in the original execution is bounded to the configured cycle count.
README instructions are complete and accurate.
